parsed_review.summary: CP00-526 is a descriptor-only RP17 AI-governance closeout pack (risk C, 150 units, RP17.P02.M08.S19-RP17.P03.M05.S22). Read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are supported and correctly scoped. Pack binding (registry.js:205-220) and contract current_pack match the baseline exactly; deliverable_distribution and micro_title_distribution match the prompt and sum to 150 (registry.js:1172-1192). All 7 required public exports exist (service.js:1601/1634; validators.js:2296/2331/2387/2408/2431) and are exercised by tests that load the real plan and contract files rather than stubs (model.test.js:140-184). The validator (scripts/validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs) deep-equals the contract against registry constants and asserts every CP526 boundary; command-evidence shows rp17:ai-governance-core:validate, npm test, and pre-review gates at exit 0. No-write/no-real-data is verified: grep found no fs writes, network, child_process, or require in the package, and every result object stamps writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false (service.js:1553-1595). Hermes appears only as descriptor packet/gate metadata with receipt_shape '..._no_write', never product runtime. Claude is read-only (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob) and explicitly not final approver (descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), with downstream human closeout steps in the hardened_review_sequence (registry.js:531). The pack does not overclaim: command-evidence and manifest report production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review; only a production_ready_candidate (coverage&slice valid) is exposed. Review hardening is intact: auth preflight passed (readiness-report exit 0, valid JSON), forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields (registry.js:518-530), no forbidden tools, and no active untracked implementation files omitted. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs) are correctly segregated and not claimed as pack scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: PASS reflects the read-only review step only; final closeout and enterprise-trust claim remain gated on post-review validation and human approval
findings.details: Per the hardened_review_sequence (packages/ai-governance/src/registry.js:531), this Claude run is the 'read_only_claude_run_with_raw_capture' step, followed by receipt_normalization, receipt_validation, finding_adjudication, construction_inspection, final_closeout_validation, and commit. Accordingly, command-evidence.json notes (line 127) that npm run closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, npm run closeout-pack-plan:generate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, and git diff --check --cached are deferred to after this review produces an accepted result; raw-output.json and review-receipt.json do not yet exist in the artifacts directory (expected at this stage). The authority boundary (claude_is_final_approver=false, human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true) and the in-code descriptor boundary (service.js:1646-1651: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true) mean this PASS does not close the pack, does not open RP17 runtime, and does not establish enterprise trust.
findings.recommendation: Treat this PASS as independent read-only review evidence only. Complete the remaining hardened_review_sequence steps (receipt normalization/validation, finding adjudication, construction inspection, final_closeout_validation including closeout-pack:validate and git diff --check --cached) and obtain human final approval before treating CP00-526 or the standing goal as closed or claiming enterprise trust / runtime readiness.
findings.post_review_disposition: expected_by_design — deferred post-review gates and human approval are documented parts of the hardened review sequence and authority boundary, not a defect. No code or evidence change required; informational only to keep the closeout boundary explicit.
